title: Warning notification when editing multiple items in a batch
excerpt:
Using Ctrl+A makes it possible to select all items on a board while looking at an open item page. Editing/changing an item on that open item page updates all the items that are selected. 
Someone on our team accidentally…

posts.author: CookieCutter (Nuunsep Dor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Using Ctrl+A makes it possible to select all items on a board while looking at an open item page. Editing/changing an item on that open item page updates all the items that are selected.

Someone on our team accidentally modified ALL the items on our board when they were adding a note to just one with Ctrl+V (paste).

Fortunately Monday.com has a feature for these mistakes: the green undo notification pops up and remains for 1 minute. Unfortunately our team member didn’t notice the “undo” button in time.

It’d be great if there was a batch change notification or warning like, “Are you sure you want to update these 349 items?”

posts.author: abremner (Amy Bremner)
posts.cookedHtml:
The batch editing is a super useful feature, but unfortunately we’ve experience huge data loss that required hours of time to restore it all. It’s too easy for someone to accidentally change or delete data, and in our case not even realise it will much later. Can we, at minimum, have a pop up window that requests confirmation for any batch editing?

“You’ve selected x number of items. Do you wish to edit all items?”

We’d also like batching to be permission based. Admins should be able to batch items, but I’d like the capability to restrict some users from this.
